frames.title: Cage Line
frames.body: Mist sits on the Chain-Road like damp wool soaked in livestock math. Iron cages rattle on ox-wains while overseers count numbers instead of names — and spit when the count comes up short. You are Nine-Mark: thrall, fighter, and cargo that punches back when the whip gets lazy.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
frames.body: Halbrecht Quill lights a match off a warrant nail and looks almost human. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. Tonight’s camp will argue theology with mosquitoes and memory. Quill packs the silence like powder — dry, useful, dangerous.

frames.title: Road Vermin Tax
frames.body: Three road vermin block a ford with a rope and a grin. Their leader — missing two fingers and all shame — offers “protection” for coin, a story, or a bruise. Quill’s hand rests near his bolt like punctuation.

choices.label: Pay the damn rope
choices.approach: Buy the ford; save steel for Vern.

choices.label: Scare them honest
choices.approach: Show the freemark and the crossbow’s opinion.

choices.label: Trade a Chain-Road story
choices.approach: Give them a tale sharp enough to sell twice.

frames.title: Quill's Ugly Joke
frames.body: At dusk Quill asks whether you want to be a freeman who hunts, or a hunter who happens to be free. The difference, he says, is who you apologize to when the smoke clears.

choices.label: Freeman who hunts
choices.approach: Keep the moral order: freedom first, warrants second.

choices.label: Hunter who is free
choices.approach: Admit the work shapes the man.

choices.label: Neither — Lyra’s man
choices.approach: Refuse Quill’s categories; keep the vow central.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ash Aside
frames.body: Steel rides light; the ledger in Quill’s coat does not. You catch yourself walking like cargo that learned a verb. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Quill drawls, “Paper freedom first. Real freedom files later.” Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

frames.title: The Binder Arrives
frames.body: A lean man in travel-stained grey tips his hat to the overseer as if haggling over mule feed and mildly disappointing weather. Halbrecht Quill — warrant binder, chain-buyer, scholar of ugly laws — pays coin for your papers without looking away from your eyes. “Pretty muscle,” he tells the overseer. “Ugly price. I’ll take him.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: Quill buys your freedom like a man purchasing a complicated knife. The next mile pretends to be empty and fails the audition. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

frames.title: Quill Names the Work
frames.body: “I buy thralls who can ruin the men who sold them,” Quill says, not unkind. “If that offends your poetry, walk. If it fits your teeth, load.” Ash loads.

frames.title: Vern's Joke
frames.body: A traveler repeats Vern’s favorite joke about unpaid tribute and burned kitchens. Nobody laughs. Quill tips the man a copper for the geography hidden in the cruelty.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men. The cane smell thickens like a dare.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Quill says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership.” Quill tips his hat at unfinished justice.

frames.title: Paper Freedom
frames.body: Quill slides a stamped sheet across the wagon board. Freedom on parchment, conditional as a hangman’s courtesy: hunt the guilty he names, keep steel sharp, ask no soft questions. “Say yes,” he offers, almost kindly, “or climb back into inventory. I don’t do poetry.”

choices.label: Take the warrant path
choices.approach: Accept conditional freedom; work for Quill.

choices.label: Bargain for a name first
choices.approach: Ask whose name this work is really for.

choices.label: Test the man's honesty
choices.approach: Watch for a twitch that says he buys thralls for worse work.

frames.body: Night fire snaps. Quill boils bitter tea that tastes like boiled warrants and asks what you will do if Lyra is already broken past rescue. The question sits between you like a third traveler with muddy boots and no manners.

frames.title: Which Warrant?
frames.body: Three warrants hang like wet laundry: a goblin raid-chief, a barge cutter on the Brand-River feeder, and a knight who sold villagers under Cade’s quiet seal. Quill lets you choose the first debt to collect. “Pick the one that keeps you sleeping,” he says. “Or the one that doesn’t. Both teach.”

frames.title: Free Horizon: Quill's Aside
frames.body: Dust counsel replaces court counsel without missing the bastard. Ash rolls a shoulder that still remembers iron’s manners. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

flagEchoes.line: Candlemire’s guest wing scratches your palm whenever you make a fist.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Cane Wind
frames.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. Somewhere a horn asks a question steel usually answers. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

flagEchoes.line: Somewhere Cade’s riders still chase a river you invented — good.
Your near-fall oiled a latch — theater as lockpick, still working.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Dust Mile
frames.body: Quill refuses to advise and means it as the highest respect. Ash keeps vows that ledgers cannot translate into inventory. Quill tips a ruined hat at unfinished justice and means goodbye.

flagEchoes.line: Freemark’s rat still flinches in stories told two towns back.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Quiet Counsel
frames.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

flagEchoes.line: Quiet passage bought with Quill’s settled debt still oils tonight’s miles.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Road Hygiene
frames.body: Remnant coin buys late knives and earlier, uglier panic. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences. Another frame turns like a dusty page with teeth.

flagEchoes.line: You refused Quill’s side warrant; Lyra-first still sits between you like weather.
Your hard hand has a reputation that arrives before your freemark does.

frames.title: Remnant Coin
frames.body: A last purse of Cade’s coin hires desperate blades on the western track. Mark-hunters who missed earlier chapters arrive late and louder. Dust settles the debate.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Old Hunger
frames.body: Steel rides light; the ledger in Quill’s coat does not. Somewhere a horn asks a question steel usually answers. Quill tips his hat at unfinished justice.

flagEchoes.line: Mud still remembers the night you chose noisy mercy over clean maps.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: Night insects keep score of every secret step you still take. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. The Wilderland watches and keeps its receipts.

flagEchoes.line: A steward’s soft knock lives in your wrist like a second pulse.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Ledger Weather
frames.body: A child’s chalk freemark on a fence post has already been scrubbed. A road-vermin kid offers directions priced in half-truths. Quill tips a ruined hat at unfinished justice and means goodbye.

flagEchoes.line: Lyra’s sale lot number ticks behind your eyes like a fuse.
Posing as Cade’s muscle left a bruise on your pride and a useful swagger.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Steel Practice
frames.body: Distant cane fields clap like soft applause for bad systems. Tonight’s camp will argue theology with mosquitoes and memory. You walk west as inventory that refused the count and won.

flagEchoes.line: River cold still lives in your bones — you beat the Brand-River once.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Night Watch
frames.body: A dead mule teaches economics better than any clerk. Mercy, fire, and shared dawn wait like weather you finally choose. The next mile does not apologize.

flagEchoes.line: Gate smiles still taste like the purse you widened on the way in.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Iron Humor
frames.body: Horizon light writes nothing but possibility — terrifying, holy. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

flagEchoes.line: Posing as Cade’s muscle left a bruise on your pride and a useful swagger.
You refused Quill’s categories — still a vow with a sword, not a category.

frames.title: Free Horizon
frames.body: The frame opens onto sky without chimneys writing orders on it. Ash and Lyra stand in weather that asks no brand. Quill waits without advising — binder to the end, friend on purpose.

flagEchoes.line: A gallery key warms against your ribs like a conspiracy with teeth.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Collar Echo
frames.body: A roadside shrine wears three names and one empty nail. The ending triad is not a trick — it is authorship with consequences. Quill tips his hat at unfinished justice.

flagEchoes.line: False papers held. Courage still holds their corners when clerks stare.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Dust counsel replaces court counsel without missing the bastard. You trade a story for water and keep the ending private. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

flagEchoes.line: The window tap — her name, your risk — still echoes under gallery silk.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Hard Mercy
frames.body: A hanged scarecrow wears Cade’s check like a joke that landed. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. You walk on because stopping is how numbers return.

flagEchoes.line: Freemark proof upstairs means Lyra is not a ghost you invented.
Quiet passage bought with Quill’s settled debt still oils tonight’s miles.

frames.title: Free Horizon: Vermin Bargain
frames.body: Distant cane fields clap like soft applause for bad systems. Last enforcers make theology out of leftover steel and lose. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

flagEchoes.line: Midnight thralls still count on the pact you whispered in kitchen steam.

frames.title: Horizon Vote
frames.body: Candlemire’s smoke thins behind you like a bad religion losing believers. Quill waits without advising — the highest respect he knows. Lyra’s quiet is not emptiness; it is a question about what kind of free you will be, and whether free gets to invent new cages.

choices.label: Take the merciful road
choices.approach: Leave leftover justice to other hunters; walk west.

choices.label: Finish hard justice
choices.approach: Burn what remains of Cade’s legal skin.

choices.label: Found a shared dawn
choices.approach: Stay and build a freemark hold that keeps no collars.

frames.title: The Merciful Road
frames.body: West opens without a brand on any skyline. Lyra walks beside you; Quill tips his hat and files no warrant for kindness. The Wilderland keeps room for people who leave cages empty.

frames.title: Hard Justice
frames.body: Candlemire’s last seals blacken. Hard justice is a fire that warms freed hands and frightens soft thieves. You do not apologize for the smoke.

frames.title: Shared Dawn
frames.body: A freemark hold rises where cane once hid screams. Shared dawn means watches, kitchens, and laws that refuse inventory of people. Quill stays to teach binders a better trade.
